meta.description:
GatorTec contact form handler — replaces Formspree.

Two webhook endpoints:
  - /webhook/contact-b2b  — receives B2B form posts
  - /webhook/contact-store — receives consumer/home form posts

For each:
  1. Webhook receives the form-encoded POST
  2. Code node parses fields, honeypot-checks, builds an RFC822 MIME message with From: b2b@/store@
  3. IF node skips on honeypot detection
  4. HTTP Request POSTs the raw message to gmail.googleapis.com/v1/users/me/messages/send using Jim's Gmail OAuth credential
     (Gmail honors the From header because b2b@ and store@ are verified Send-As aliases on Jim's account)
  5. Respond node returns 303 redirect to /contact/thanks.html

Email shape:
  From:     GatorTec B2B <b2b@gatortec.com>  (or 'GatorTec Store <store@gatortec.com>')
  To:       customer
  Cc:       b2b@ (or store@) — internal copy
  Reply-To: customer email
  Subject:  'New B2B inquiry — Company (Name)' or 'New consumer inquiry — Name'
  Body:     Plain text with all form fields

Setup:
  1. Import this JSON
  2. Confirm the Gmail OAuth credential is Jim's account (credentials id may differ in your instance)
  3. Activate the workflow
  4. Test with a real form submission from the site

Why HTTP Request instead of the Gmail node: n8n's Gmail node in some versions doesn't expose the 'From' header for Send-As aliases. Calling the Gmail REST API directly via HTTP Request gives us full control of the message envelope — the OAuth credential authenticates as jim@, but the From header can be any verified alias (b2b@ or store@).
